label: Slate-Rafter Bedchamber
description: Slate-Rafter Bedchamber forms the enclosed sleeping room of The Surveyor's Loft, set beneath a low roof of soot-dark rafters braced by riveted iron straps. A narrow cot-frame and a lidded cedar chest occupy most of the floor, with wall-niches cut into the repaired masonry for folded linens and sealed papers. The single slit window is sunk in a deep embrasure, its stonework scored with measure-marks and smeared with the lime dust that pervades Cindervale’s survey regimen. Plaster partitions and blackened brick arches define the room’s tight geometry, giving it the disciplined austerity of a frontier administrative residence.
